title: GFP-VSV Infection
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bgeso","text":"-Aliquot of GFP-VSV virus (from -80°C) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e43q9","text":"-Serological pipets","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61k1h","text":"-1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ei89u","text":"-Serum free DMEM media (high glucose) [depending on cell type]","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfvh4","text":"-DMEM media (high glucose + 10% FBS) [depending on cell type]","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cft53","text":"-Ice bucket","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: If infecting transfected cells, infect them 24 hours after transfection.
Replace media with fresh media (with FBS) prior to infection with GFP-VSV.

Dilute VSV-GFP in serum-free culture media. Perform the necessary calculations to determine the dilution that is required to obtain the desired MOI (multiplicity of infection). Note: MOI is the number of viruses/number of cells. For example, a MOI of 1 = 500,000 PFU (plaque forming units) of virus per 500,000 cells. Note: We typically start with a dilution range of MOI = 0.01, 0.1, 1 for optimization
Infect cells by dropping the diluted virus directly into the wells making sure to disperse the virus around the well.
Gently swirl the plate to help disperse the virus.
Leave the plate for approximately 24 hours in a 37°C CO 2 incubator. Then proceed with endpoint analysis.

Sample calculation : (MO1 = 1; Virus titre = 8.4x10 8 virus/mL ; 500,000 cells/well;) Step 1: calculate amount of PFU per well Step 2: calculate how much to dilute the viral stock C 1 V 1 =C 2 V 2 Where V 1 = volume/well you have C 1 = virus/mL calculated in step 1 C 2 = virus titre (stock concentration) V 2 = x (unknown volume to be calculated) You will add x mL of your virus stock per well. This volume will be low, and thus must be diluted in media for easier pipetting.
Thaw VSV-GFP stock aliquot on ice
creator: Kelsey Grimes
